rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Acco Engineered Systems, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Monokote fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to Carlton Seyforth's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Charles Ay's declaration and to plaintiff's disputed facts nos. 7 and 8 are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
